privileged_info_summary: The ellipse_fill@1 task uses synthetic data: a 600x400 pixel image generated by ImageMagick with a gray border on white background. The task requires creating a red-filled circular selection in the center area of the image. Verified constraints include: red color detection using RGB(>=200, <=50, <=50), red coverage between 5-40% of image area (12000-96000 pixels), circular shape with aspect ratio >=0.7, center positioning in the middle 60% of the image (x:120-480, y:80-320 for 600x400), and pass threshold at 75% (3/4 criteria). The environment is GIMP 2.10 running on Ubuntu with 1920x1080 screen at 10 FPS observation rate. CRITICAL FINDING: README claims minimum filled area of 800 pixels, but verifier code only enforces minimum of 100 pixels—a significant discrepancy suggesting the README overstates the size requirement.
